---
title: "About"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

## Project Description

This project studies how fertility distributions shape sibling and broader kin-count distributions. Using statistical models of fertility and family structure, we estimate empirical fertility patterns, derive sibling distributions, and simulate relative counts across relationship categories.

## Methods

### Fertility Modeling

We use a zero-inflated negative binomial (ZINB) distribution to model fertility patterns:

$$P(X = k) = \begin{cases}
\pi + (1 - \pi) \cdot NB(0; \mu, \theta) & \text{if } k = 0 \\
(1 - \pi) \cdot NB(k; \mu, \theta) & \text{if } k > 0
\end{cases}$$

where:
- $\pi$ is the zero-inflation probability (excess childlessness)
- $\mu$ is the mean of the negative binomial component
- $\theta$ is the dispersion parameter

### Sibling Distribution

The sibling distribution is derived from the fertility distribution, using the relationship: $$P(num_{sib}=k) \propto (k+1)P(num_{child}=k+1).$$

## Software

This project uses:

- R for statistical computing, with `pscl` for zero-inflated models and `ggplot2` for visualization
- Quarto for the methods and analysis website
- React, Vite, and Recharts for the interactive [Kincounts simulator](https://kincounts.vercel.app/) (deployed on Vercel)
- A legacy Shiny app, kept for local use

## Authors

This research is conducted at the Lasisi Lab.

## Contact

For questions or collaborations, please visit our [GitHub repository](https://github.com/lasisilab/kincounts).
